speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, brother Richard, Lord Hastings, and the rest:

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yet thus far Fortune maketh us amends,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And says that once more I shall interchange

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: My wanèd state for Henry’s regal crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Well have we passed, and now re-passed, the seas,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And brought desirèd help from Burgundy.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What then remains, we being thus arrived

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: From Ravenspurgh Haven before the gates of York,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But that we enter as into our dukedom?

speaker: RICHARD
text: The gates made fast? Brother, I like not this.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For many men that stumble at the threshold

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are well foretold that danger lurks within.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Tush, man, abodements must not now affright us.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: By fair or foul means we must enter in,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For hither will our friends repair to us.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: My liege, I’ll knock once more to summon them.

speaker: MAYOR
text: My lords, we were forewarnèd of your coming,

speaker: MAYOR
text: And shut the gates for safety of ourselves,

speaker: MAYOR
text: For now we owe allegiance unto Henry.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But, master mayor, if Henry be your king,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yet Edward, at the least, is Duke of York.

speaker: MAYOR
text: True, my good lord, I know you for no less.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, and I challenge nothing but my dukedom,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: As being well content with that alone.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But when the fox hath once got in his nose,

speaker: RICHARD
text: He’ll soon find means to make the body follow.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Why, master mayor, why stand you in a doubt?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Open the gates. We are King Henry’s friends.

speaker: MAYOR
text: Ay, say you so? The gates shall then be opened.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A wise stout captain, and soon persuaded.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The good old man would fain that all were well,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: So ’twere not long of him; but being entered,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I doubt not, I, but we shall soon persuade

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Both him and all his brothers unto reason.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: So, master mayor, these gates must not be shut

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But in the night or in the time of war.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What, fear not, man, but yield me up the keys.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For Edward will defend the town and thee

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And all those friends that deign to follow me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, this is Sir John Montgomery,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our trusty friend, unless I be deceived.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Welcome, Sir John. But why come you in arms?

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: To help King Edward in his time of storm,

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: As every loyal subject ought to do.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Thanks, good Montgomery. But we now forget

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Our title to the crown, and only claim

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Our dukedom, till God please to send the rest.

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: Then fare you well, for I will hence again.

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: I came to serve a king and not a duke.—

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: Drummer, strike up, and let us march away.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Nay, stay, Sir John, a while, and we’ll debate

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: By what safe means the crown may be recovered.

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: What talk you of debating? In few words,

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: If you’ll not here proclaim yourself our king,

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: I’ll leave you to your fortune and be gone

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: To keep them back that come to succor you.

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: Why shall we fight if you pretend no title?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, brother, wherefore stand you on nice points?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: When we grow stronger, then we’ll make our claim.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Till then ’tis wisdom to conceal our meaning.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Away with scrupulous wit! Now arms must rule.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And fearless minds climb soonest unto crowns.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, we will proclaim you out of hand;

speaker: RICHARD
text: The bruit thereof will bring you many friends.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Then be it as you will, for ’tis my right,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And Henry but usurps the diadem.

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: Ay, now my sovereign speaketh like himself,

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: And now will I be Edward’s champion.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Sound, trumpet! Edward shall be here proclaimed.—

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Come, fellow soldier, make thou proclamation.

speaker: SOLDIER
text:
reads
            
            
              
              Edward
               
              the
               
              Fourth
              ,
               
              by
               
              the
               
              Grace
               
              of
              
              God
              ,
               
              King
               
              of
               
              England
               
              and
               
              France
              ,
               
              and
               
              Lord
               
              of
              
              Ireland
              ,
               
              &c
              .

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: And whosoe’er gainsays King Edward’s right,

speaker: MONTGOMERY
text: By this I challenge him to single fight.

speaker: ALL
text: Long live Edward the Fourth!

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Thanks, brave Montgomery, and thanks unto you all.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: If fortune serve me, I’ll requite this kindness.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now, for this night let’s harbor here in York,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And when the morning sun shall raise his car

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Above the border of this horizon,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: We’ll forward towards Warwick and his mates;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For well I wot that Henry is no soldier.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ah, froward Clarence, how evil it beseems thee

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: To flatter Henry and forsake thy brother!

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Yet, as we may, we’ll meet both thee and Warwick.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Come on, brave soldiers; doubt not of the day;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And that once gotten, doubt not of large pay.
